title: Polestar Promises Tariff-Avoiding Local Vehicle Production Strategy
text: Polestar implements manufacturing diversification strategy to mitigate tariff risks. Currently manufactures EVs across multiple locations with Polestar 1 and 2 in China, Polestar 3 in South Carolina, and Polestar 4 in South Korea. Plans future production including Polestar 4 in South Korea and Polestar 7 in Europe. CEO Michael Lohscheller stated Polestar plans to move more manufacturing to Europe as U.S. tariffs and global trade war spell trouble for automotive industry. In Europe, brand faces 29.3% tariffs on imported cars with potential battery pack tariffs. Localized production strategy aims to reduce dependence on Chinese manufacturing and qualify for U.S. EV tax credits. Unlike EV startups that struggled, Polestar accesses Geely's capital resources and manufacturing footprint enabling more effective implementation. Regional production represents key part of Polestar's future approach to navigating global trade dynamics.
